did.design: Treatment: QA-passing routes with Spirit share >= 15% in 2026Q1 (last full pre-shutdown quarter). Control: matched on 500-mile distance bucket + closest mean daily passengers, zero Spirit share 2025Q2-2026Q1. Registered quantity: treatment-minus-control fare spread, Q3 2026 vs Q1 2026 baseline.

events.detail: Berkshire Hathaway's 13F (filed Nov 14, 2016) reveals new stakes in American, Delta, United, and Southwest — a famously un-diversified investor joins the common-ownership pool at scale.
events.label: Berkshire buys into all four majors

events.detail: US passenger volumes fall ~95% at the trough. Fares and route networks convulse; every series on this chart shows the scar.
events.label: COVID-19 collapses air travel

events.detail: Buffett announces Berkshire sold its entire ~10% stakes in all four majors during April 2020 ('The world has changed for the airlines'). A large undiversified-ish cross-holder leaves the pool — watch MHHI delta step down.
events.label: Berkshire exits all airline stakes
events.source: Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting, May 2, 2020; CNBC 2020-05-02

events.detail: Frontier goes public but remains a controlled company under Indigo Partners — index funds hold only a sliver. Not every airline is index-owned alike.
events.label: Frontier IPOs (Indigo keeps control)

events.detail: A federal judge blocks the $3.8B acquisition on antitrust grounds, removing Spirit's exit ramp and setting up its bankruptcy.
events.label: JetBlue–Spirit merger blocked
events.source: US v. JetBlue, D. Mass., Jan 16 2024

events.detail: Alaska Air Group closes its acquisition of Hawaiian Airlines — ordinary consolidation, visible in HHI on the routes they shared.
events.label: Alaska absorbs Hawaiian

events.detail: Spirit files a prearranged Chapter 11 converting ~$795M of notes to equity.
events.label: Spirit files Chapter 11 (first)

events.detail: Old shareholders (including the index funds) are wiped out; former bondholders — Citadel, PIMCO, Arena, Cyrus, Ares — take the equity. Spirit leaves the common-ownership pool while still flying. The index funds had already lost this one.
events.label: Spirit emerges — creditors own it now

events.detail: Five months after emerging, Spirit is back in bankruptcy — this time headed for liquidation.
events.label: Spirit files Chapter 11 (second)

events.detail: The Iran-war fuel spike raises costs on ALL routes — the confound the difference-in-differences design exists to remove.
events.label: Jet-fuel price spike

events.detail: The natural experiment begins: a major low-cost carrier exits ~200 routes at once. Concentration jumps; the common-ownership term barely moves (Spirit was creditor-owned). The registered prediction starts its clock.
events.label: Spirit Airlines ceases operations

events.detail: Two weeks after Spirit's shutdown, Berkshire discloses a $2.6B Delta stake — the cross-holding web starts rewiring itself before the experiment even reports.
events.label: Buffett returns: Berkshire takes 6.1% of Delta
events.source: Berkshire SC 13G on DAL, May 2026; CNBC 2026-05-15

sources.fares: DOT Consumer Airfare Report Table 6 (Socrata yj5y-b2ir)

regimeContext.decomposition: DGS Tables III-IV: placebo ownership + true market shares still yields a significant positive coefficient (0.212), while true ownership + placebo market shares yields ~0 or negative (-0.144) — their case that the correlation lives in the market-share component.
regimeContext.framework: O'Brien & Salop (2000), Antitrust Law Journal 67:559 — the MHHI framework.
regimeContext.refutation: AST, 'A Refutation of...' (SSRN 4158149, 2022); DGS, 'A Surrebuttal...' (SSRN 4307814, 2022). The fight remains open.
regimeContext.replication: DGS first replicate AST's market-level estimate almost exactly (0.196 vs 0.202; DGS Table II Panel B) before their corrections — the disagreement is about assumptions, not data errors.

regimes.citation: Azar, Schmalz & Tecu (2018), 'Anticompetitive Effects of Common Ownership', J. Finance 73(4):1513-1565, Table 3 col. (6): market-level, full controls, 2001Q1-2014Q4.
regimes.label: AST (2018) — common ownership raises fares

regimes.citation: Dennis, Gerardi & Schenone (2022), 'Common Ownership Does Not Have Anticompetitive Effects in the Airline Industry', J. Finance 77(5):2765-2798, Table V Panel B col. (4): IV specification, market-level. Coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero.
regimes.label: DGS (2022) — the effect is statistically zero
